product_overview: Peek provides AI-generated, personalized home-screen widgets that synthesize calendar, task, and weather data into beautiful at-a-glance visuals. The backend offers authenticated data aggregation, AI-driven HTML generation, and screenshot rendering to produce widget images for mobile clients.
core_goals: Generate contextual, personalized widget content using user integrations (Google Calendar, Todoist) and weather data via AI.
Provide a scalable pipeline to convert AI-generated HTML into PNG widget images using a screenshot/rendering service.
Expose clear, authenticated APIs for generating single and batch widget outputs while enabling integration management and previews.
Allow users to manage integrations and widget layouts from the dashboard with robust auth and health checks.

features.name: AI-Generated Personalized Widgets (Full generation pipeline)
features.description: Generate a complete widget image using the user's connected integrations and AI-driven content/styling decisions.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/* (e.g., /api/auth/signin) with Google OAuth credentials -> Receive 200/302 and session cookie or auth response -> POST /api/generate with { width, height, layout, date, currentTime, timezone, location } and session cookie -> Receive 200 { url: string } -> GET {url} to download generated widget image -> Receive 200 image/png
POST /api/generate with valid body but without session/auth -> Receive 401 Unauthorized error

POST /api/auth/* (sign-in) with valid credentials -> Receive session -> POST /api/generate with valid body referencing enabled integration sources -> Receive 200 { url: string } -> If AI or integrations fail, POST /api/generate returns 500 Server error JSON

features.name: Widget HTML Generation API
features.description: Produce the widget HTML string using real user data and AI so clients can preview or render it themselves.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/* (signin) with valid credentials -> Receive session cookie -> POST /api/generate/html with { layout, date, currentTime, timezone, location } and session cookie -> Receive 200 text/html string
POST /api/generate/html without authentication -> Receive 401 Unauthorized error

POST /api/auth/* (signin) -> Receive session -> POST /api/generate/html with valid body -> Receive 200 text/html string -> POST /api/screenshot with { width, height, layout: 'generated-html', data: { html: '<...>' }, useMockData: false, scale: 2 } -> Receive 200 image/png

features.name: Screenshot Generation API
features.description: Render widget HTML/layout data to a PNG image using Puppeteer and HTML templates. This endpoint is used to produce final widget images or previews.
user_flows: POST /api/screenshot with { width, height, layout, variant, data, useMockData, scale } (valid schema) -> Receive 200 image/png
POST /api/screenshot with missing required fields (e.g., no width) -> Receive 400 Validation error JSON
POST /api/generate/html with valid request -> Receive 200 text/html string -> POST /api/screenshot with that layout/data to produce image -> Receive 200 image/png

features.name: Authentication (Better Auth: Google, Todoist, email/password)
features.description: Sign-in, sign-up, and session management via Better Auth to enable access to user-specific data and protected endpoints.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/* (e.g., /api/auth/signin) with Google OAuth code -> Receive 302 redirect / 200 with session cookie -> POST /api/trpc/viewer.me with session cookie -> Receive 200 { id, name, email, image }
POST /api/auth/* with invalid OAuth code or bad credentials -> Receive 400/401 Auth error
GET /api/auth/* (session introspection) with invalid/expired cookie -> Receive 401 or session-not-found response

features.name: tRPC - Viewer (current user)
features.description: Provide the currently authenticated user's profile for UI personalization and generation pipelines.
user_flows: POST /api/trpc/viewer.me with valid session cookie -> Receive 200 { id, name, email, image }
POST /api/trpc/viewer.me without session cookie -> Receive 401 UNAUTHORIZED error

features.name: tRPC - Widget Layouts
features.description: List available widget layout options (IDs and labels) for clients to present gallery and selection UIs.
user_flows: POST /api/trpc/widgets.layouts -> Receive 200 array of { id, label } (public, returns all layouts)
POST /api/trpc/widgets.layouts during server misconfiguration or template load error -> Receive 500 Server error JSON

features.name: tRPC - Integrations Management
features.description: Manage OAuth provider connections (Google Calendar, Todoist): list providers, get details, toggle individual sources (calendars/projects), and disconnect providers.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/* (signin) -> Receive session cookie -> POST /api/trpc/integrations.list with session cookie -> Receive 200 Array<{ id, name, icon, connected, sources }>
POST /api/trpc/integrations.list without session (public) -> Receive 200 with empty or non-user-specific data (note: endpoint is public but returns empty for unauthenticated users)
POST /api/auth/* (signin) -> Receive session -> POST /api/trpc/integrations.detail with { provider } and session cookie -> Receive 200 { id, name, connected, sources }
POST /api/trpc/integrations.detail without session cookie -> Receive 401 UNAUTHORIZED error
POST /api/auth/* (signin) -> Receive session -> POST /api/trpc/integrations.toggleSource with { sourceId, enabled } and session cookie -> Receive 200 updated source object
POST /api/trpc/integrations.toggleSource with invalid sourceId -> Receive 400 Validation error JSON
POST /api/auth/* (signin) -> Receive session -> POST /api/trpc/integrations.disconnect with { provider } and session cookie -> Receive 200 Success
POST /api/trpc/integrations.disconnect without session cookie -> Receive 401 UNAUTHORIZED error

features.name: tRPC - Batch HTML Generation
features.description: Generate HTML strings for multiple widget layouts in a single authenticated request to minimize AI calls and support batch rendering.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/* (signin) -> Receive session cookie -> POST /api/trpc/generate.batchHtml with { layouts: ["layoutId1","layoutId2"], date, currentTime, timezone, location } and session cookie -> Receive 200 Record<string, string> mapping layoutId to generated HTML
POST /api/trpc/generate.batchHtml without session cookie -> Receive 401 UNAUTHORIZED error
POST /api/trpc/generate.batchHtml with valid session -> Receive 200 mapping -> For each returned HTML: POST /api/screenshot with layout and corresponding data -> Receive 200 image/png per layout

features.name: tRPC - Health Check
features.description: Lightweight health endpoint to report server status and authentication state for monitoring and client fallbacks.
user_flows: POST /api/trpc/meta.health -> Receive 200 { ok: boolean, authenticated: boolean, serverTime: string, source: string }
POST /api/trpc/meta.health during outage -> Receive 500 Server error JSON

features.description: Renders widget screenshots from provided data using Puppeteer and HTML templates

endpoints.description: Generate a widget screenshot PNG from layout data

endpoints.description: Better Auth handler for all auth routes (login, callback, session, etc.)

Returns server health status and authentication state

known_limitations.issue: Screenshot API has no authentication, anyone can generate screenshots
known_limitations.impact: Public endpoint could be abused for resource consumption

known_limitations.issue: tRPC integrations.list is a public procedure but returns user-specific data
known_limitations.impact: Returns empty data for unauthenticated users but does not enforce auth
